threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think you can navigate by Tube when it’s strike season. Welcome to the chaos, mate.
Last night on the 59, a guy was selling homemade samosas. Smelled amazing and surprisingly, no one complained about the bus being late.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a guy casually playing the saxophone at 3 a.m. on my street, surrounded by a crowd of laughing neighbors—it felt like home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin can you find a Kiosk serving currywurst until 4 AM, making it the perfect late-night pit stop after clubbing.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know a local when they can name at least three food trucks that used to be in that spot on 16th before they got pushed out.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 5 bus today, and a guy serenaded us with an impromptu opera performance. Only in the Tenderloin can a commute come with a show.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all nights end at 11, but in Gràcia, the real party starts just after midnight—many miss out on the late-night vermut vibes.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a new arrival by how they always look puzzled at the tram stops, waiting for lines that don’t exist on weekends.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You can spot newcomers by their confused faces when the 73 bus skips stops without warning, like it’s playing hide and seek.
Last night on the 59, a lad tried to impress a girl by reciting poetry, but he totally fluffed the last line and everyone burst out laughing.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I found a street musician playing bachata on a cold Tuesday evening, and everyone just stopped to dance. That’s when I knew I was home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do you find kebab shops open till 4 AM serving fresh bread while the U-Bahn grinds to a halt. A true late-night staple.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only a true local knows to stash an umbrella in their bag, not for rain, but for the inevitable pigeon poop landing when you least expect it.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the Muni at Civic Center today, it stopped for a good ten minutes because a dog decided to take a nap on the tracks. Just another Tuesday.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all the local bars serve 'tapas', but in reality, many just serve 'pinxos'. It's a whole different vibe, and the locals know which places get it right.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their tendency to stand on the wrong side of the escalator in Diagonal metro, oblivious to the rush.
